title: sanguisorba minor subsp. maroccana
intro: Sanguisorba minor subsp. maroccana, also known as Moroccan burnet, is a subspecies of the Sanguisorba minor plant, which is a perennial herb belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to stop bleeding and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Moroccan burnet include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate symptoms of digestive issues such as diarrhea and ulcers. Moroccan burnet can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, making it a versatile and valuable herb for various health applications.
